grids_layouts.safeArea: Always use useSafeAreaInsets from react-native-safe-area-context for screens to prevent content overlapping notches and home indicators.

patterns.card: backgroundColor: theme.surface, borderRadius: 8, padding: 16, borderWidth: 1, borderColor: theme.border
patterns.denseList: Use FlatList with ItemSeparatorComponent (1px border height). Remove heavy cards for high density.

components.button: Min height 48px for tap targets. Pill or rounded-md (8px). Use TouchableOpacity with activeOpacity={0.7}. Add icons from @expo/vector-icons. Do not use generic text - use bold text.
components.input: Height 48px. 1px solid border. paddingHorizontal 16. Focus state must change border color to Primary.
components.switch: Standard RN Switch with trackColor={{ false: theme.border, true: theme.primary }}
components.listItem: Height min 64px. Flex row. Left: Avatar/Icon, Center: Title + Subtitle, Right: Status indicator or Action. Symmetric spacing.
components.logsTerminal: ScrollView inside a View with backgroundColor: '#000' (even in light theme for terminal feel). Monospace font, color success/danger/warning/primary for specific log levels. padding: 12.
components.bottomTabs: Height 60px + safe area bottom. Minimalist icons (Ionicons or Feather). No background transparency, pure solid surface color.

motion.transitions: Use react-native-reanimated for smooth layout transitions (e.g. Layout.duration(200).springify() for list items).
motion.interactions: Provide haptic feedback for critical actions using expo-haptics (e.g., starting farm, saving fingerprint, license error).

accessibility.testIDs: CRITICAL: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a testID attribute (React Native equivalent of data-testid). Use kebab-case role-based names (e.g., testID='login-submit-button').
accessibility.tapTargets: Min 48x48. Wrap small icons in View with hitSlop={{ top: 10, bottom: 10, left: 10, right: 10 }} if visual size must be smaller.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the Android mobile app using React Native elements and Expo Router.
Use standard StyleSheet.create for all styling. DO NOT use Tailwind CSS or web HTML tags (div, span).
CRITICAL: Create files as .js or .jsx exclusively unless a .tsx file already exists and must be modified.
Maintain the density and functionality of the desktop application (HWID license check, single/multi profile warmup, fingerprint configuration, proxy settings).
Utilize dark/light theme logic using React Native's useColorScheme.
For logs and terminal outputs, use purely monospace fonts, dark backgrounds, and distinct colors for log levels (green for success, red for error, yellow for warning).
All tap targets must be minimum 48px or utilize hitSlop.
Use react-native-reanimated for layout animations (e.g., list item insertion/deletion).
Never use generic default alerts; build customized modal components or inline error states.
Make sure to map desktop structures from Python (ui.py, fingerprint.py) to a clean React Native mobile interface.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Do not converge toward generic outputs. Design a unique, high-contrast, professional tech interface.
Do not use dark colors as gradients. Stick to solid dark backgrounds (e.g. #09090B).
Create depth through nested elements and defined borders, not just shadows, as shadows on Android can be buggy without elevation.
Never use AI assistant Emoji characters (like 🤖🧠) for icons. Use @expo/vector-icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations (e.g., use TouchableOpacity, or Reanimated).
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable within components, but keep dense lists compact for data visibility.
Use sonner or a React Native equivalent (like react-native-toast-message) for toast notifications if needed, otherwise build a simple animated toast.
Prioritize using functional React components and standard RN Hooks.
Components MUST use named exports; Pages MUST use default exports.
